title.zh: 綠色霸權的陰影：匈牙利電池巨廠背後的政治博弈
title.en: The Green Mirage: Hungary's Battery Boom and the Far-Right Political Ecology

summary.zh: 匈牙利政府近年高調推動電動車產業鏈建設，試圖借電動車轉型浪潮躍升為全球第二大電池製造國。這項政策背後隱藏著深層的政治算計——右翼政府將氣候變遷否定論轉化為「偽環保主義」，用綠色名義掩蓋經濟擴張與民族主義動員。然而，這場被稱為「CATL德布勒森 Gigafactory」的巨型電池工廠爭議，卻引發了社會激烈分裂：支持者與反對者皆以民族主義、排外言辭爭奪民意，甚至將生態議題扭曲為政治攻訐的工具。文章指出，這種「極右政治生態學」並非真為環境，而是將生態議題工具化，透過製造「敵人」強化自身立場，重現了執政聯盟Fidesz-KDNP慣用的「我們vs他們」恐懼營銷策略。
summary.en: Hungary’s far-right government has aggressively positioned itself at the heart of the electric vehicle transition, aiming to become the world’s second-largest battery manufacturer. This ambition, however, is less about genuine climate action and more about leveraging pseudo-environmentalism to advance economic expansion and nationalist mobilization. The proposed CATL gigafactory in Debrecen—a centerpiece of Hungary’s industrial strategy—has ignited fierce societal polarization. Supporters and opponents alike deploy exclusionary rhetoric, framing the debate through nationalist and xenophobic lenses. The article reveals how both sides exploit environmentalism as a tactical facade, weaponizing ecological concerns to serve non-environmental agendas. This phenomenon, termed ‘far-right political ecology,’ hinges on strategic enemy-creation: by invoking fear of external threats, authorities and critics alike reinforce an ‘us versus them’ narrative, echoing the rhetorical playbook of the ruling Fidesz-KDNP coalition.

keyArticle.title: Far right political ecologies and the battery sector: The case of the CATL gigafactory in Debrecen, Hungary
